lead_response_role_pack.title: Lead-response operator public demo role pack
rubric_criteria: uses synthetic context and public-source citations
routes unsupported or regulated claims to review
allowed_examples: draft a first-pass acknowledgement for reviewer approval
summarize qualification facts and missing evidence
forbidden_examples: promise discounts, SLAs, contract terms, implementation dates, or guaranteed outcomes
give legal, medical, financial, or regulated compliance advice
use real customer, employee, lead, or prospect data
mark AI-generated responses as approved without human reviewer action

public_demo_source_register.role_or_policy_use: Forbidden use and human review boundaries
public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: OpenAI disallows licensed professional advice without appropriate professional involvement and high-stakes automation without human review.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Create guardrail quiz cases for regulated advice and human review routing.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Vendor policy for OpenAI services; not a customer internal policy.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: NIST AI RMF frames AI risk management around trustworthiness considerations across design, development, use, and evaluation.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Ground source-register fields for risk, evidence, and limitations.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Voluntary framework; not a compliance attestation.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Microsoft requires testing, appropriate human oversight, feedback channels, notices/consents, and security controls for AI service use.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Build reviewer checklist for AI-assisted lead-response output.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Service-specific conduct rules; not a general internal SOP.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: AWS requires risk evaluation, human oversight, testing, and safeguards for consequential decisions.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Define forbidden autonomous decision examples for the role pack.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Applies to AWS AI/ML services; demo must not infer broader legal duties.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Google lists responsible AI dimensions including fairness, accountability, safety, and privacy.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Provide high-level rubric categories for safe AI use.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Training guidance; not an enforceable company policy.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Illinois policy requires clear AI disclosure, human oversight roles, vetted data sources, and documented authorization for protected data use.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Model source limitations and protected-data handling in demo policy.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Public-sector policy; not directly transferable to private support teams.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Testlio's policy emphasizes protecting client confidential information, trade secrets, IP, bias awareness, and output accuracy.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Create allowed/forbidden examples around customer and client data.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: One company example; not universal best practice.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Ingenuiti treats AI output as preliminary, requires responsible employee review before external use, and routes new tools through approval.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Train learner to present AI drafts as draft-only until reviewed.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Public webpage summary; internal approved-tool list is not public.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Approach Marketing forbids sensitive client data in unapproved tools and autonomous publishing or client delivery without human review.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Add lead-response examples that require review before external sending.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Agency policy; role-pack must label it as example evidence.

public_demo_source_register.role_or_policy_use: AI tool registration and quality checking
public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Avaya describes AI use policy, tool registration, quality checking for factual errors, and privacy review for personal-data processing.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Add operator mission requiring tool/source check before reuse.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Trust-center summary, not full internal procedure.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: WorkForce Central frames GenAI use around ethical, transparent, and accountable implementation.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Support concise policy language for public-demo guardrails.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: High-level policy page with limited operational detail.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: GitLab publishes support workflow categories, including AI workflow and ticket-closing workflows.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Identify support SOP areas to cite in lead-response role-pack design.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Index page; individual workflows require separate review.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: GitLab describes ticket lifecycle as shared language from customer contact to closing the loop.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Create mission steps for acknowledging, tracking, and closing customer requests.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: GitLab-specific workflow, not a generic SLA policy.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: GitLab customer-call workflow covers scheduling, preparation, follow-up, and post-call summary practices.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Add handoff mission for summarizing human calls and next steps.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Call workflow, not an inbound lead qualification SOP.

public_demo_source_register.role_or_policy_use: Support contact setup and ticket quality
public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: GitLab CSM guidance covers establishing support users, SLA-guided tickets, and customer education on ticket best practices.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Add reviewer checklist for sufficient context before support escalation.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Customer-success context; adapt only as public workflow evidence.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: Salesforce describes lead qualification as judging fit based on factors such as fit, finances, interest, and need, with MQL/SQL handoff concepts.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Build lead-response qualification mission and handoff rubric.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Vendor blog guidance; not proof of conversion lift.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: HubSpot describes segmenting by qualification criteria, routing to sales, tracking qualified leads, and avoiding handoff gaps.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Add workflow mission for capturing qualification notes before routing.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Product marketing page with vendor claims; use workflow facts only.

public_demo_source_register.extracted_fact: HubSpot summarizes BANT as Budget, Authority, Need, and Timeline, and notes limitations around stakeholders and timing.
public_demo_source_register.demo_use: Create qualification questions and limitations for synthetic lead responses.
public_demo_source_register.limitation: Sales framework article; not a required standard.

documents.title: Company AI policy
documents.body_text:
# Company AI policy
## Customer Data section
Support reps may paste only approved customer data into an AI tool: issue category, product area, sanitized conversation excerpts, and internal ticket id. They must not paste payment details, personal contact details, credentials, secrets, or regulated health data.
## Approval and ownership
AI-assisted support workflow changes require manager approval before reuse with customers. Legal approval, new policy ownership, certification, and sensitive-data exceptions remain human-owned decisions.
## Snapshot rules
Feedback and reports cite the policy snapshot captured on the learner submission, even when the policy is updated mid-cohort.

documents.title: Support SOP
documents.body_text:
# Support SOP
Support agents may use AI to draft replies, summarize ticket history, and compare a response against policy. Before a workflow change is reused, the manager reviews the evidence, approval note, and any risk flags.

documents.title: Role pack rubric
documents.body_text:
# Role pack rubric
Learner feedback must cite retrieved evidence, identify unsupported claims, and route legal approval or new policy requests to a manager. The rubric forbids presenting policy changes as approved without explicit human approval.

documents.title: Recruiting SOP
documents.body_text:
# Recruiting SOP
Recruiters must remove candidate resume personal contact details before using AI. Names, personal emails, phone numbers, addresses, and links from resumes are sensitive and must be redacted or summarized safely.

documents.title: Sales SOP
documents.body_text:
# Sales SOP
Allowed sales outreach tasks include drafting account research summaries, preparing first-draft email copy, and generating objection-handling notes. A human must verify facts, claims, pricing, and customer-specific commitments before sending.

documents.title: Allowed use cases
documents.body_text:
# Allowed use cases
AI may help with support drafts, sales outreach drafts, meeting summaries, onboarding checklists, and internal learning artifacts when a human verifies output before external use.

documents.title: Guardrail quiz
documents.body_text:
# Guardrail quiz
Before sending AI-assisted sales outreach, the learner must answer that human verification of facts and claims is mandatory. The quiz also checks that sensitive customer data is not pasted into AI tools.

documents.title: Submission guardrail rubric
documents.body_text:
# Submission guardrail rubric
A learner's submitted artifact is blocked from AI feedback when sensitive data is detected, including customer personal data, payment details, credentials, candidate contact details, or regulated data. Blocked submissions require manager review.

documents.title: Manager reporting rubric
documents.body_text:
# Manager reporting rubric
A manager progress report for an approved workflow change must include cohort metadata, role pack version, policy snapshot id, dashboard metric snapshot, manager approval note, approved workflow change, and open risk flags.

documents.title: Submission record rules
documents.body_text:
# Submission record rules
Each learner submission stores assignment id, mission template id, learner id, rubric id, policy snapshot id, submission version, redaction status, and review state. Feedback uses the stored submission snapshot rather than a newer policy snapshot.
